summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 3: re-detect against main (22 bugs resolved in rounds 0-2). Fan-out by domain cluster, seen-filtered (22 sigs), adversarial verify + classify. Stop condition: zero new framework bugs for 2 consecutive rounds (Round 2 was NOT clean, so a clean Round 3 = round 1 of 2).
logs: Round 3 detection: 6 from clusters = 6 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 3 verify: 6 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: packages/fields/src/Concerns/HasValidation.php:148 getValidationRules() omits nullable for optional typed fields -> ResourceController::validated() 422 on JSON null
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Optional typed fields reject JSON null on save (no `nullable` injected) — every non-required Date/DateTime/Email/Url/Number/etc. field 422s when its empty value is submitted as JSON null
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In HasValidation::getValidationRules(), after assembling $resolved, if the field is not required (no 'required' key) and has no 'nullable' key but does carry at least one type/default rule, prepend 'nullable' (e.g. array_key_exists checks on the normalized keys). Equivalent alternative: FieldRulesExtractor::extract() prepends 'nullable' for any field whose rule set lacks 'required'/'nullable'. Either reconciles the bare type rule with the JSON null that the framework's own typed React inputs (DateInput/DateTimeInput/NumberInput/SelectInput/nativeFields) emit on clear.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end on current main. (1) Field level: new DateTimeField('published_at')->getValidationRules() === ['date'] and new EmailField('email')->getValidationRules() === ['email'] — no nullable injected anywhere (verified base Field.php and Resource.php carry no nullable/sometimes default). (2) Laravel behavior: via Validator, bare type rules ['date','email','url','numeric','integer','boolean','string','array'] all FAIL on explicit JSON null while accepting '' and missing key. (3) Full chain through the real showcase Resources + Arqel\Form\FieldRulesExtractor->extract(effectiveFields()) which feeds ResourceController::validated()'s $request->validate($rules): with ALL required fields filled, payload {published_at:null} -> 422 'The published at field must be a valid date.'; Author {name:'X',email:null} -> 422 'The email field must be a valid email address.'; email:'' passes. This is the exact path the controller runs. Showcase PostResource line 86 (new DateTimeField('published_at')) and AuthorResource line 44 (new EmailField('email')) are NOT ->required() and NOT ->nullable(), so leaving them empty is legal, documented usage. Decisive non-theoretical evidence: the framework's OWN React inputs emit JSON null on clear — DateInput.tsx:38 and DateTimeInput.tsx:30 (onChange(value===''?null:value)), NumberInput.tsx:28/57 (onChange(null)), SelectInput.tsx:36, CurrencyInput.tsx:61, and ui/src/form/nativeFields.tsx:172/228/324/347 all normalize empty to null. Inertia serializes that as JSON null, not ''. So a user who clears or never fills an optional typed field genuinely cannot save. Classification = framework-bug (not app-misuse): although raw Laravel would 422 the same way, the framework ships BOTH halves — null-emitting typed inputs AND null-rejecting typed default rules — with no reconciliation, and the documented Field API treats ->nullable() as a manual opt-in. The two framework-controlled ends are mutually inconsistent against the documented pattern the showcase follows. Not a deferred feature (no ticket defers this; unrelated to EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL surface). Severity high not blocker: required-only records still save, and a per-field ->nullable() workaround exists, but every optional Date/DateTime/Email/Url/Number/Select/File field across any app is affected and silently breaks normal create/edit flows. Baseline php artisan test stays 8/8 green — no smoke test submits a typed field as null, so the gap is genuinely uncovered.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: `HasValidation::getValidationRules()` (packages/fields/src/Concerns/HasValidation.php:148-173) merges `getDefaultRules()` (e.g. DateTimeField -> ['date'], EmailField -> ['email'], UrlField -> ['url'], NumberField -> ['numeric'|'integer'], ImageField/FileField -> ['image'|'file'|'array']) with explicitly-added rules but NEVER prepends `nullable` for fields that are not ->required(). So an optional field's effective rule set is just the bare type rule. Every Laravel type rule REJECTS an explicit JSON null: verified via tinker that null fails ['date'],['email'],['url'],['numeric'],['integer'],['boolean'],['string'],['array'],['image'],['file'] (all fails=true). Inertia/React typed inputs (date picker, etc.) send JSON null — not '' — for a cleared/empty optional value, so this fires in normal use. Reproduced END-TO-END via real HTTP through Arqel\Core\Http\Controllers\ResourceController::validated() which runs `$request->validate($rules)` with rules from FieldRulesExtractor->extract(effectiveFields()): (1) POST /admin/posts {published_at: null, ...required ok} -> HTTP 422 {"published_at":["The published at field must be a valid date."]}; (2) PUT /admin/posts/{id} clearing published_at -> same 422; (3) POST /admin/authors {name:'X', email:null, bio:null} -> HTTP 422 {"email":["The email field must be a valid email address."]}. Confirmed PostResource published_at is NOT required and Author email is NOT required, so leaving them empty is legal app usage. Note '' (empty string) is leniently accepted by Laravel for some rules, but typed React inputs emit null, so users genuinely cannot create a Post without published_at nor clear an existing optional date/email. The rules extractor itself is fine; the defect is in the per-field rule resolution. Showcase baseline (php artisan test) stays green at 8/91 — the existing smoke tests never submit a typed field as null, so this gap is uncovered. Fix: getValidationRules() (or each typed getDefaultRules() consumer) should inject 'nullable' when 'required' is absent, OR FieldRulesExtractor should prepend nullable for non-required fields. Affected emitters: packages/fields/src/Types/{DateField,DateTimeField,EmailField,UrlField,NumberField,ColorField,ImageField,FileField,SelectField,BooleanField,SlugField}.php.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Tenant switching does not persist with SessionResolver — switchTo() writes to a user DB column it never reads back
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Override SessionResolver::switchTo($user, $tenant) to persist into the session: $session->put($this->sessionKey, $tenant->getKey()) (resolve the session via app('session')/the current Request facade since the SupportsTenantSwitching::switchTo signature does not receive the Request), instead of inheriting AbstractTenantResolver's user-DB-column write. Add a test exercising switchTo -> resolve round-trip on the same session store.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced and confirmed against current main. SessionResolver::resolve() reads the active tenant from a session key (default 'current_tenant_id'), but SessionResolver does NOT override switchTo() and so inherits AbstractTenantResolver::switchTo() (lines 140-149), which writes $user->current_tenant_id = $tenant->getKey() and $user->save() to the USER DB column — never touching the session. grep across the entire tenant package (packages/tenant/src) finds ZERO session writes (no session()->put anywhere), so when SessionResolver is the active resolver nothing ever populates the key it reads. The controller flow (TenantSwitcherController::switch -> TenantManager::switchTo at TenantManager.php:239-249 -> resolver->switchTo + in-memory set()) makes the switch visible only for the current request; redirect()->intended fires, and on the NEXT request SessionResolver::resolve re-reads the unchanged session and the switch is silently lost. This is not intended/deferred behavior: it directly contradicts the canonical spec PLANNING/09-fase-2-essenciais.md:755 ('Para session resolver, update session') and line 130 (SessionResolver reads session key current_tenant_id), and the SupportsTenantSwitching contract docblock lists 'session entry' as the expected mechanism for such a resolver. SessionResolver is shipped as a first-class concrete resolver (SKILL.md:18) and is config-selectable via arqel.tenancy.resolver (TenantServiceProvider::buildConfiguredResolver), so any app choosing it gets a broken switch. The asymmetry is real: AuthUserResolver::resolve() reads the tenant via currentTeam backed by the current_tenant_id FK, so the inherited DB-column switchTo round-trips correctly there — which is exactly why the existing TenantSwitcherTest (AuthUserResolver) passes while the SessionResolver switchTo path is entirely uncovered (SessionResolverTest only tests resolve()). Classified framework-bug, not app-misuse: both showcase and tenant-demo use AuthUserResolver so the baseline is unaffected, but the defect is in the shipped framework resolver, not in app usage. Severity high (not blocker): complete functional break of one of five shipped resolvers contradicting the spec with no test coverage, but not triggered by the baseline apps.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/tenant/src/Resolvers/SessionResolver.php resolves the current tenant by reading a session key (`$request->session()->get($this->sessionKey)`, default 'current_tenant_id'), but it does NOT override switchTo(). It therefore inherits AbstractTenantResolver::switchTo() (packages/tenant/src/Resolvers/AbstractTenantResolver.php:140-149) which does `$user->{current_tenant_id} = $tenant->getKey(); $user->save();` — writing to a column on the USER model and never touching the session. TenantManager::switchTo() (packages/tenant/src/TenantManager.php:239-249) delegates to the resolver and then set()s the in-memory tenant, so the change is visible only for the remainder of the current request. The TenantSwitcherController::switch flow (packages/tenant/src/Http/Controllers/TenantSwitcherController.php:27-42, mapped at packages/tenant/routes/admin.php as POST admin/tenants/{tenantId}/switch) redirects after switching; on the NEXT request SessionResolver::resolve re-reads the unchanged session key and the switch is silently lost. AbstractTenantResolver's user-column persistence is correct only for AuthUserResolver (which is what the existing TenantSwitcherTest exercises); the SessionResolver path is uncovered and broken. PathResolver/SubdomainResolver/HeaderResolver inherit the same user-column switchTo, equally meaningless for those URL-driven resolvers.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: AiCache::get() returns null for objects stored via put() on database/file cache drivers (serializable_classes=false ⇒ __PHP_Incomplete_Class); cache never hits, cost double-counted
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: AiCache is non-functional on the default `database` cache driver — get() returns null after put(), defeating all AI response caching and double-counting cost
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/ai/src/AiCache.php:59 (put stores raw AiCompletionResult object) and :42-44 (get relies on `$value instanceof AiCompletionResult`)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In AiCache::put() store a plain array snapshot (e.g. `(array) $result` / explicit ['text'=>...,'inputTokens'=>...,'raw'=>...]) and in get() rehydrate via `new AiCompletionResult(...)` when the cached value is an array, instead of relying on native object serialization that Laravel's default database/file cache driver rejects when cache.serializable_classes=false.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced on current main against apps/showcase (Laravel 13.14.0, stock CACHE_STORE=database, stock config/cache.php with serializable_classes=false). Direct probe: Cache::store('database')->put($key, new AiCompletionResult(...)) then ->get($key) returns a __PHP_Incomplete_Class (raw DB value is a valid O:27:"Arqel\\Ai\\AiCompletionResult" serialization), so AiCache::get()'s `$value instanceof AiCompletionResult` check is false and it returns null — confirmed via CacheManager::getSerializableClasses (cache.php:473) passing false into DatabaseStore, whose unserialize() (DatabaseStore.php:593-594) calls unserialize($value, ['allowed_classes' => false]). Differential confirmed: the same object round-trips fine on the array driver (instanceof true), which is exactly why packages/ai/tests/Unit/AiCacheTest.php passes — phpunit.xml pins CACHE_STORE=array, masking the bug in CI. Causal chain to cost double-counting verified by reading AiManager::complete() (lines 53-65): a null cache get skips the short-circuit, re-invokes the provider, and unconditionally calls CostTracker::record() which does AiUsage::query()->create() (CostTracker.php:60) — so every repeated identical prompt inserts another ai_usage row and re-bills. AiCache::has() still returns true (key exists), so callers cannot detect the silent miss. This is not app-misuse: serializable_classes=false and CACHE_STORE=database are both stock Laravel 13 defaults (verified against vendor/laravel/framework/config/cache.php and the unmodified showcase config), and nothing in the ai package SKILL/README documents this limitation. It is unrelated to the deferred export signed-URL surface (EXPORT-007/008). Severity high (not blocker): the caching/cost-tracking feature is entirely non-functional and silently over-bills on the default driver, but it does not crash requests or corrupt data, and a non-default cache driver or serializable_classes allowlist would mask it.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: AiCache (packages/ai/src/AiCache.php) stores an `AiCompletionResult` object via `Cache::store()->put()` and on get() checks `$value instanceof AiCompletionResult`. On the showcase's default driver (CACHE_STORE=database, Laravel 13.14), Laravel's `DatabaseStore::unserialize` (vendor/.../Cache/DatabaseStore.php:594) calls `unserialize($value, ['allowed_classes' => $this->serializableClasses])`, and `cache.serializable_classes` defaults to `false`. Live probe: after `AiCache::put('p',['o'=>1],$res)`, `Cache::store('database')->get($key)` returns a `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` (raw stored value is valid: `O:27:"Arqel\Ai\AiCompletionResult":6:{...}`), so `instanceof` is false and `get()` returns null. End-to-end via AiManager::complete() with the showcase StubProvider: two IDENTICAL `complete('Tagline for {product}',['temperature'=>0.5])` calls produced TWO `ai_usage` rows (rows_after_1=1, rows_after_2_cachehit=2) — the cache NEVER hits, every repeated prompt re-invokes the provider and re-records cost. Confirmed driver-specific: same serialize/unserialize works in-process and on the `array` driver (which is why the package's AiCacheTest passes under Testbench's array driver — the bug is masked in tests). `AiCache::has()` still returns true (key exists), so callers cannot detect the miss. Fix belongs in the framework: AiCache should persist a plain array snapshot of AiCompletionResult and rehydrate it, instead of relying on native object serialization that the framework's own default cache driver rejects.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Widget polling + deferred fetch never fire: PHP payload omits dashboardId and uses 'pollingInterval' while WidgetRenderer reads 'dashboardId' and 'poll'
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In Dashboard::resolve(), inject the parent id into each serialized widget (e.g. map $widget->toArray($user) + ['dashboardId' => $this->id, 'widgetId' => $widget->id()]) AND rename the polling key so JS sees it — either emit 'poll' => $this->pollingInterval in Widget::toArray() or change WidgetRenderer to read widget.pollingInterval. Also fix WidgetRenderer's widgetId fallback: 'widget.name' must become the full id() (type:name) or findWidget() will 404.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced by code inspection on current main (apps/showcase baseline). PHP Widget::toArray() (Widget.php:242) serialises 'pollingInterval' and never emits 'dashboardId', 'widgetId', or 'poll'; Dashboard::resolve() (Dashboard.php:302-303) maps each widget via toArray() without injecting the dashboard id. WidgetRenderer.tsx reads widget.dashboardId (line 50, always undefined), widget.poll (line 52, always 0 because PHP sends pollingInterval), and guards the effect with `if (!dashboardId || !widgetId) return` (line 59). With dashboardId permanently undefined, BOTH the deferred fetch (lines 87-89) and the polling interval (lines 91-95) are dead code: deferred widgets emit data:null from PHP (Widget.php:245) and never fetch, staying permanently blank; poll>0 is never reached. DashboardGrid.tsx (lines 84-91) has dashboard.id in props but passes the raw widget without injecting it, and ArqelDashboardPage.tsx forwards the payload unchanged — no remapping exists anywhere in packages-js/types/src or apps. A third, deeper mismatch: even if dashboardId were present, the widgetId fallback `widget.widgetId ?? widget.name` (line 51) yields the bare name (e.g. 'revenue'), but the controller's Dashboard::findWidget (Dashboard.php:346) matches on $widget->id() = type:name (e.g. 'stat:revenue'), so the refetch would 404. The unit test WidgetRenderer.test.tsx:67-76 hand-builds poll/dashboardId/widgetId keys and stubs the fetcher, masking both the payload-key mismatch and the findWidget id mismatch; the PHP WidgetTest.php:93-96 only asserts 'pollingInterval', confirming the PHP contract and the absence of any boundary test for the keys JS actually reads. This is distinct from the prior envelope-unwrap (lines 75-78) + withFilters() fix, which are present and correct. It is a framework bug, not app-misuse: showcase's MainDashboard.php doesn't currently call ->deferred() or ->poll() (so the baseline renders fine via the inline path), but RF-W-04 (Polling) and RF-W-05 (Deferred) are non-functional end-to-end for any consumer. Severity high (not blocker) because the default inline-data path works and the baseline app is unaffected; not the deferred export-signed-URL surface (EXPORT-007/008) — this is live widget wiring.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: PHP Arqel\Widgets\Widget::toArray() (packages/widgets/src/Widget.php:231-247) serialises the widget with keys 'id', 'pollingInterval', 'deferred', 'data' — it never emits 'dashboardId', 'widgetId', or 'poll'. Dashboard::resolve() (packages/widgets/src/Dashboard.php:298-311) calls $widget->toArray($user) and likewise never injects the parent dashboard id into each widget. On the JS side, packages-js/ui/src/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx reads: line 50 `const dashboardId = widget.dashboardId;` (always undefined), line 51 `const widgetId = widget.widgetId ?? widget.name`, line 52 `const poll = typeof widget.poll === 'number' ? widget.poll : 0` (always 0, since PHP emits 'pollingInterval'). The effect guards at line 59 `if (!dashboardId || !widgetId) return undefined;` so with dashboardId always undefined, BOTH deferred fetch (line 87-89) and polling (line 91-95) are dead code — deferred widgets emit data:null from PHP and never fetch, staying permanently blank; poll>0 is never reached. DashboardGrid.tsx (line 84-91) passes the raw `widget` to WidgetRenderer without injecting dashboard.id (which it has in props). No remapping exists in packages-js/types/src, ArqelDashboardPage.tsx, or apps/showcase. The unit test packages-js/ui/tests/widgets/WidgetRenderer.test.tsx:73-75 hand-builds payloads with `poll: 5, dashboardId: 'overview', widgetId: 'kpi'`, masking the real-payload mismatch (7 tests pass). RF-W-04 (Polling) and RF-W-05 (Deferred) are non-functional end-to-end in production. Distinct from the prior 'envelope + filters' fix (the envelope unwrap at lines 75-78 and withFilters() are present and correct; the dashboardId/widgetId/poll wiring was never connected).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: transitionTo() mutates+saves state without calling TransitionAuthorizer or checking transition from(); guard only affects UI flag
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: HasWorkflow::transitionTo() never consults TransitionAuthorizer — illegal/unauthorized transitions are executed and persisted unconditionally
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In transitionTo(), before mutating in the non-spatie fallback branch, resolve the matching transition class for (fromKey -> newState) from the WorkflowDefinition and enforce it: reject if no transition declares the target reachable from the current state (from() eligibility), and call TransitionAuthorizer::authorize($transitionClass, Auth::user(), $this) — throwing an UnauthorizedException (or returning false) when denied. e.g. guard the `else { $this->{$field}=$newState; $this->save(); }` block so illegal/unauthorized transitions are not persisted.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely on current main using the package's own test harness (sqlite in-memory, WorkflowOrder + DeniedTransition fixtures): (1) TransitionAuthorizer::authorize(DeniedTransition) returns false — the deny-by-default guard works; (2) $order->transitionTo(PaidState::class) from Pending still mutates and persists to PaidState despite the authorizer denying it; (3) transitionTo(ShippedState::class) executes an illegal Pending->Shipped skip with no from() eligibility check. Root cause: HasWorkflow::transitionTo() lines 121-126 either delegates to spatie's transitionTo (which keeps spatie guards) or, in the duck-typed fallback path used by raw-string/enum/slug models (the package's primary positioning, e.g. showcase Ticket), does `$this->{$field}=$newState; $this->save()` with zero authorization or eligibility check. Grep confirms TransitionAuthorizer::authorize() has exactly ONE caller: StateTransitionField.php:307, where it only computes a read-only UI `authorized` flag. This is a framework bug, not app-misuse: showcase uses the documented HasWorkflow/transitionTo() API exactly as shown in SKILL.md, and the package ships NO other server-side enforcement point — the Http\Controllers\TransitionController that SKILL.md (WF-010) claims 'valida + dispara transições' does not exist in src/ (no Http/ dir, no routes registered by WorkflowServiceProvider). It is not a deferred feature: WF-006 (marked delivered) has the explicit acceptance criterion 'Server-side check prevents unauthorized calls'. So the package's deny-by-default authorizer — its WF-006 centerpiece — is unreachable from any mutation path it ships, leaving users believing enforcement exists when it does not. Severity medium (not low: it's a security-relevant authorization-bypass of the package's own guard invariant; not high: the spatie-cast path retains spatie guards, and an app can layer its own gate before calling transitionTo).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: TransitionAuthorizer::authorize() (packages/workflow/src/Authorization/TransitionAuthorizer.php) is the package's deny-by-default guard, but grep shows it is ONLY called from StateTransitionField::isAuthorized() to compute a UI display flag (packages/workflow/src/Fields/StateTransitionField.php:307). `HasWorkflow::transitionTo()` (packages/workflow/src/Concerns/HasWorkflow.php:107-145) takes a target-state string and, for the non-spatie fallback path, does `$this->{$field} = $newState; $this->save();` with no `from()` eligibility check and no authorizer call before mutating. Live probe on showcase Ticket (states open/in_progress/resolved): `$t->transitionTo('resolved')` from `open` succeeded and persisted (status open→resolved, history row written) despite skipping in_progress — and would equally execute a transition the authorizer would deny. Server-side guard/authorization enforcement claimed by the package's deny-by-default authorizer is bypassed entirely at the execution entry point; only the read-only field flag honors it. Borderline (the trait is documented as a thin helper delegating enforcement to gates in the HTTP layer), but reported because guards do NOT reject illegal transitions at transitionTo().

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: StatWidget secondary description never renders: PHP data() emits key 'description' but StatCard reads 'statDescription' (and 'description' clobbers the widget chrome subtitle)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In StatWidget::data() change the emitted key from 'description' to 'statDescription' (i.e. `'statDescription' => $this->resolveStatDescription()`) so it matches the StatCard TS contract; update StatWidgetTest.php assertions (lines 34,38,45) from data()['description'] to data()['statDescription'].
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced the cross-package contract mismatch end-to-end on current main. PHP Arqel\Widgets\StatWidget::data() emits the secondary comparison line under key 'description' (packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php:171), while the JS StatCard reads only 'statDescription' (packages-js/ui/src/widgets/StatCard.tsx:106,114,122) and its TS interface (line 30) declares statDescription?. There is no transform/rename layer between them (grep confirms statDescription never appears in any PHP source or controller). Consequences: (1) the secondary line ('+12% vs last week') NEVER renders because the payload has no statDescription key; (2) WidgetRenderer.mergeData uses {...widget, ...data} (WidgetRenderer.tsx:140), so data.description clobbers the chrome subtitle widget.description (from Widget::toArray():239) passed to WidgetWrapper's description prop (StatCard.tsx:144) — one mismatch breaks two fields. Intent is unambiguous: StatCard's own docblock (lines 6-10) lists 'description?' AND 'statDescription?' as separate keys, the base Widget chrome owns 'description', and the StatWidget PHP setter is named statDescription(). The PHP unit test asserts data()['description'] (StatWidgetTest.php:34,38,45) and the JS test hand-builds both keys separately (StatCard.test.tsx:28), so both test suites pass while masking the integration gap — neither exercises the real PHP→JS payload. This is a framework defect, not app-misuse: showcase MainDashboard doesn't even call statDescription(), so the user does nothing wrong; any app using the documented ->statDescription() feature gets a silent no-op plus a clobbered subtitle. Same bug class as prior fixed key-name mismatches. Not the export signed-URL surface (EXPORT-007/008) and not a deferred feature. Severity medium: an advertised, documented feature silently fails with a side-effect, but dashboards still render core values so it is not a blocker.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: PHP Arqel\Widgets\StatWidget::data() (packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php:167-178) emits the comparison line under key 'description': `'description' => $this->resolveStatDescription()`. The fluent setter is named statDescription() (line 105), and its own PHP test asserts data()['description'] (packages/widgets/tests/Unit/StatWidgetTest.php:34,38,45). On the JS side, StatCard.tsx destructures `statDescription` (line 106) and only renders the secondary line via `{statDescription && (...)}` (line 114-124) using statDescription (line 122) — the key 'statDescription' is never present in the real payload, so the secondary line ('+12% vs last week') never renders. The StatCard docblock itself (lines 6-10) claims data() returns 'statDescription?', confirming that is the intended key and PHP's 'description' is the bug. Compounding: WidgetRenderer.mergeData (WidgetRenderer.tsx:138-143) does `{...widget, ...data}`, so the stat's data.description overwrites the widget chrome's widget.description, meaning WidgetWrapper's `description` prop (StatCard.tsx:144) receives the stat comparison text instead of the chrome subtitle, while the statDescription slot stays empty — one key-name mismatch breaks two fields. JS test StatCard.test.tsx:28 hand-builds `statDescription: '+12% vs last week'`, masking the mismatch.
